meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Scale Your Freelance Workflow (2025 Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project scaling. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end apps faster for your clients.
content.answer_block: To overcome scaling bottlenecks, adopt a modular SaaS starter kit architecture. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, context-aware implementation, you reduce boilerplate by 80%. This approach shifts your focus from repetitive setup to high-value feature delivery, allowing you to ship production-ready SaaS apps in days.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, another client project is delayed, and you’re stuck re-writing authentication and database schemas for the tenth time. You aren't just building software; you're trapped in a cycle of manual configuration that kills your profit margins and your sanity. The gap between a talented freelancer and a scalable agency is the ability to leverage standardized, high-velocity infrastructure.

Imagine a world where your project boilerplate is pre-validated, modular, and ready to deploy the moment you sign a contract. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous feature implementation, you transform your workflow from a manual grind into a high-octane production line. It's time to stop building from scratch and start scaling your impact.

steps.title: Define the Modular Architecture
steps.description: Establish a base repository structure that separates Auth, Payment processing, and UI components into isolated, reusable modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular SaaS scaffold structure for Next.js, separating features into /src/modules with dedicated auth, billing, and profile directories.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize folder structure based on the modular SaaS blueprint provided by Antigravity'

steps.title: Automate Authentication Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't write login logic by hand. Generate a robust, secure authentication layer that handles JWTs and session management instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure NextAuth configuration with Google and Email providers, including database schema integration for Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement NextAuth with Prisma adapter and secure session middleware'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Use your starter kit to inject a pre-built billing flow that handles subscriptions, webhooks, and usage-based pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler and subscription management service that maps to our existing User model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Connect Stripe webhooks to the database and update user subscription status'

steps.title: Contextual UI Component Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your design system to specific pages, ensuring consistency across every client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dashboard layout using Shadcn UI components that consumes our modular API services.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Build a responsive dashboard page using the generated UI components and hook into the user auth state'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Perform an automated audit of your new codebase to ensure type safety and production readiness before client handoff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a comprehensive audit on the current codebase for security vulnerabilities, type errors, and performance bottlenecks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit codebase and fix all TypeScript errors detected in the modular services'

pro_tips: Always store your starter kit in a private template repo; use 'git pull --rebase' to keep your core updated without breaking client forks.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your API routes immediately after creation to ensure your starter kit remains bulletproof.
Standardize your database naming conventions across all projects so you can share migration scripts between different client builds.
Implement a 'Feature Flag' system in your base kit to toggle expensive services like AI APIs or third-party analytics per client budget.
Treat your starter kit like a product; every time you build a custom feature for a client, refactor it into the starter kit for future reuse.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is refined, begin deploying it across your next three freelance projects to identify bottlenecks in the deployment pipeline, then iterate on your module library.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start mastering your output. By standardizing your stack, you reclaim hours of billable time and deliver enterprise-grade quality. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
